zotero.abstractNote: Large language models (LLMs) are often poorly calibrated: their confidence scores do not reflect the true probability of correctness. Existing calibration methods typically require access to the model's logit outputs or a labeled calibration dataset, which are often unavailable for proprietary models. We propose a novel calibration method that relies solely on the model's generated text. Our approach, which we call Generation-based Calibration (GenCal), uses the model's own generations to estimate its confidence. We show that GenCal significantly improves the calibration of various LLMs across a range of tasks, including question answering and summarization, without requiring any additional training or access to the model's internal states.
